source: synthetic expected-result fixture for the reputation-weighted defense; see agent_docs/expected_akash_reputation_results.md

config.weight_use: drop f lowest-weight clients first, then mean of remaining

expected_band.band_semantics: low = oracle (Byz always trimmed by zero weight), mid = realistic, high = degraded (Byz weight beats honest -> wrong trim)
expected_band.notes: Weighted TrimMean drops the lowest-weight client first. In oracle mode this is deterministic Byzantine removal. In realistic mode w_byz=0.30 < w_honest=0.85 still trims correctly. In degraded mode w_byz=0.70 > w_honest=0.65 trims an honest client instead -- the inversion that makes the high band 0.15 instead of staying low.

comparison_to_baseline.publishability_threshold: AkashRep+TrimMean ASR mid <= AkashRep+FedAvg ASR mid
